conversations.content: Let's work through a chemistry exercise in the world of Azeroth to test your ability to apply the new rule.
Using the decomposition rule for carbon dioxide in Azeroth that we covered earlier: besides one carbon atom, what else is produced when one carbon dioxide molecule decomposes?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>Decomposition rule of carbon dioxide in the world of Azeroth</content>

Relevant memory fragment: [mem-id:305] Time: 2025-06-18-16:20, Content: The user informed the AI that in the world of Azeroth, one carbon dioxide molecule decomposes into one hydrogen molecule, one carbon atom, and one oxygen molecule.

conversations.think: The memory contains one relevant entry. [mem-id:305] clearly states the decomposition rule for carbon dioxide in Azeroth: one carbon dioxide molecule decomposes into one hydrogen molecule, one carbon atom, and one oxygen molecule. Excluding the carbon atom, one hydrogen molecule and one oxygen molecule are also produced.
conversations.content: According to the rules of Azeroth, one hydrogen molecule and one oxygen molecule are also produced.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
